name: project-structure
observations: Monorepo managed with Turborepo
Apps located in /apps directory (web-app-1 through web-app-4, 3d-portfolio, award-winning-website)
Shared packages in /packages (ui, present, dashcraft, tailwind-config, eslint-config, typescript-config)
Custom MCP tools in /tools directory (20+ tools)
Legacy app in /legacy-app directory (plain JS)

name: tech-stack
observations: React + Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
Redux for state management
shadcn/ui for component library base
pnpm as package manager
Vitest for testing
Storybook for component stories

name: component-patterns
observations: Components in packages/ui/components/{Name}/{Name}.tsx
Each component has index.ts barrel export
Types in separate types.ts file when needed
Tests use Vitest with .test.ts extension
Stories use Storybook with .stories.tsx extension

name: mcp-tools
observations: component-factory: generates React components from shadcn/ui templates
memory: stores project context across sessions (this tool)
Multiple custom tools in /tools directory
Tools built with TypeScript and compiled to /build directories

name: review-and-fix-workflow
observations: Production-grade review→fix→review pipeline for React components
component-reviewer MCP tool analyzes TypeScript, React patterns, accessibility, performance, security, code quality, testing

component-fixer MCP tool applies fixes based on review JSON: type-safety (any→unknown, !→?.), accessibility (alt, aria-label, role, tabIndex), performance (inline functions/objects), code-quality (console.log removal, magic numbers), security (hardcoded secrets, innerHTML)
review-and-fix CLI chains both: review → fix → re-review with score delta
CLI usage: node tools/cli-wrappers/build/review-and-fix-cli.js packages/ui/components/ComponentName
Stdin usage: component-reviewer path | review-and-fix --stdin
FixEngine class handles line-based transformations with 4 fixTypes: remove, replace, add, refactor
Fixer accepts reviewJson parameter for pre-computed review results

name: Storybook Story Patterns
observations: Stories should include all component variants with proper args
Use argTypes with control: 'select' for variant/size options
Include interactive stories like AllVariants, AllSizes for visual comparison
For compound components (Dialog, Select, Tooltip), use render() with full composition
Import sub-components explicitly for proper autodocs generation
Use TooltipProvider wrapper for Tooltip stories
Include edge cases: disabled states, loading states, fallback states

name: UI Components Review Results
observations: 11 components scanned, average score 66/100
Main issues: incomplete stories, missing accessibility tests, TypeScript errors
Fixed stories for: Button, Badge, Avatar, Dialog, Select, Tooltip
PostCSS config added to packages/ui for Tailwind v4 support
Storybook main.ts updated to read stories from components/ directory
Storybook preview.ts updated to import Tailwind styles

name: UI Components Story Enhancement Complete
observations: Enhanced stories for 24 components in @repo/ui

Components enhanced: Button, Badge, Avatar, Dialog, Select, Tooltip, Card, Accordion, Alert, Checkbox, Table, DropdownMenu, HoverCard, Popover, Progress, Skeleton, Sheet, Tabs, Separator, Drawer, RadioGroup, Switch, Slider, Textarea
Each component now has comprehensive stories with: Default state, All variants, Edge cases (disabled, loading), Realistic usage examples, Proper args and controls
Fixed Storybook Tailwind v4 integration by adding postcss.config.js and importing styles in preview.ts
Fixed Storybook stories path to read from components/ directory
All stories use proper composition patterns for compound components
Input already had decent stories so was not modified

name: @repo/utils Critical Fixes
observations: Fixed XOR cipher in encrypted-storage.ts - replaced with Web Crypto API (AES-GCM with PBKDF2 key derivation)
Fixed LRU cache bug in memory-cache.ts - added createdAt timestamp for proper eviction ordering
Fixed duplicate types - Nullable/Optional/Maybe now re-exported from utility-types.ts
Fixed deepMerge mutation in object/helpers.ts - now creates new object instead of mutating target
Created AsyncTypedStorage interface in storage/types.ts for async encryption operations
Tests pass for memory-cache (10 tests) and object helpers (18 tests)
Pre-existing TypeScript errors exist in codebase (Set iteration, Zod types, duplicate exports)

name: @repo/utils Production-Grade Improvements
observations: Completed all 17 batches of improvements across 80+ files in packages/utils
Batch 1: Critical Security - Replaced XOR cipher with AES-GCM encryption (Web Crypto API), fixed LRU cache eviction bug with createdAt timestamp, removed duplicate types, fixed deepMerge mutation
Batch 2: Core Edge Cases - Added null/undefined/empty array guards to array module, NaN/Infinity handling to number module, edge cases to string module
Batch 3: Search & Filters - Sanitized highlightTag parameter for XSS prevention, added null guards to filter predicates

Batch 4: SSR Safety - Added typeof window/document guards to 7 hooks: use-local-storage, use-window-size, use-online-status, use-click-outside, use-key-press, use-visibility-change, use-copy-to-clipboard
Batch 5: Clipboard & Media - Added SSR guards to clipboard.ts, audio-player.ts, video-player.ts, hls-player.ts with error handling
Batch 6: Date Module - Added isValid checks to all date helpers, formatters, and validators
Batch 7: Error & Retry - Already well-structured, no changes needed
Batch 8: Logger & Cache - Added peek() and stats() to memory-cache, added withContext() to logger
Batch 9: Object Module - Added edge case handling to dot-notation.ts, fixed Set iteration in comparison.ts
Batch 10: Pagination - Added validation for NaN/invalid inputs to all pagination helpers
Batch 11: Performance - Added race condition fix to memoizeAsync with pending map, input validation
Batch 12: Storage - Added key validation and error logging to local-storage.ts
Batch 13: URL Module - Added empty string guards to query-params, builders, validators
Batch 14: Validation - Added empty string guards to validators, improved sanitizeInput and validateFileUpload
Batch 15: Auth - Added SSR guard to sso.ts initiateLogin, validation to auth-helpers, guards, rbac
Batch 16: API - Added baseUrl/path validation, timeout error handling, WebSocket SSR guard
Batch 17: Constants - No changes needed, already well-structured
Added tsconfig.json target: ES2022 to fix regex flag errors

name: Docker Setup
observations: Docker setup completed for development with hot-reload
All 6 web apps have Dockerfile.dev files
docker-compose.yml orchestrates all services
Apps use Vite + React with pnpm workspaces
Port mapping: web-app-1→3001, web-app-2→3002, web-app-3→3003, web-app-4→3004, 3d-portfolio→3005, award-winning-website→3006
Volume mounts enable hot-reload in containers
Docker is for dev only; production deploys to Netlify
Shared packages (ui, utils, tailwind-config) are source-only, no build step needed

name: Docker Configuration
observations: Docker setup uses turbo prune --docker for optimized builds
Development Dockerfiles (Dockerfile.dev) use multi-stage: prune -> dev
Production Dockerfiles use multi-stage: prune -> builder -> runner with nginx
docker-compose.yml uses named volumes for node_modules to persist dependencies
Source code is mounted via volumes for hot reload in development
Apps: web-app-1, web-app-2, web-app-3, web-app-4, 3d-portfolio, award-winning-website
Package manager: pnpm 8.15.6 with corepack
.dockerignore excludes node_modules, .turbo, dist, .env files

name: Turborepo Monorepo
observations: Root path: /Users/nishantchaudhary/Desktop/my-turborepo
Root package.json name: with-vite
Package manager: pnpm@8.15.6, Turbo: ^2.8.20
6 apps in apps/web/, 7 packages in packages/, 30+ MCP tools in tools/
pnpm-workspace.yaml includes apps/*, apps/web/*, packages/*, tools/*

name: 3d-portfolio naming
observations: Directory is apps/web/3d-portfolio but package name is three-d-portfolio-2025
Always use package.json name field for turbo --filter and turbo prune

name: pnpm Docker patterns
observations: NEVER share node_modules volumes across services - pnpm virtual store symlinks break
@types/node must be aligned across all apps/packages to ^24.x.x
node:20-alpine always needs libc6-compat for glibc compatibility

name: Remaining code issues
observations: packages/ui has dozens of unused imports (TS6133, noUnusedLocals: true) blocking web-app-1/2/3 prod builds
Files affected: AspectRatio, Badge, Calendar, ContextMenu, Dialog, Drawer, etc.
Fix: remove unused imports or set noUnusedLocals: false in packages/ui/tsconfig.json
web-app-3 OOM: @repo/present tsup needs ~1.5 GB RAM, Docker Desktop at 3.8 GB
Fix OOM: increase Docker Desktop memory to 8 GB+ or build web-app-3 alone

name: web-app-4 CRA special case
observations: Uses react-scripts@5.0.1, NOT Vite
Port 3000, not 5173
Production output: build/ not dist/
ENV HOST=0.0.0.0 and ENV CI=false needed in Dockerfile

name: CI/CD Pipeline
observations: GitHub Actions CI workflow created at .github/workflows/ci.yml
Runs on push to main/develop branches and pull requests
Steps: checkout, setup pnpm, setup Node.js with cache, install deps, lint, build
Uses pnpm caching for faster installs
Leverages Turborepo for monorepo build orchestration
Test step is commented out - enable when tests are added
No Remote Caching configured yet - add later if needed
Enhanced CI workflow with Turbo caching for .turbo folder
Enabled test step to run Vitest across all packages
Added --affected flag to only build changed packages
Commit 1157b0d: Enhanced workflow with Turbo cache, tests, and --affected flag

name: GitHub Actions
observations: Free tier: 2,000 minutes/month for private repos
Workflow files live in .github/workflows/
Runs on Ubuntu VMs (ubuntu-latest)
Supports caching with actions/cache or built-in cache parameter
